creator: Carpetsmoker@rwxrwxrwx.net
text:
Hi,

You committed this port a few days ago, you made a few changes to the patch I
sent, in particular you changed the s|/etc/mara|${ETCDIR}| and the rc.d
scripts back to as they were with the previous version.

Does this port still work for you? Because for me it now wants to use
/usr/local/maradnsdns and the rc.d scrips don't work because maradns now needs
a -f argument for the config file (As far as I could find there is no built-in
default, at least not in MaraDns.h -- Plus the new startup script has the
advantage you can use a different config file if one wishes).

So unless my FreeBSD system is somehow very odd, I would say this port is
broken now :( Could you please take a look at it and fix it? Thanks.

-- 
Martin Tournoij
carpetsmoker@rwxrwxrwx.net  | (+031) 621 991 576
http://www.carpetsmoker.net | http://www.daemonforums.org

QOTD:
When it's dark enough you can see the stars.
		-- Ralph Waldo Emerson
